questions.question: Design a half-digit decimal adder using Verilog HDL. Implement the adder using a 4-bit binary to decimal converter, a carry generator, and a sum register. Also, include a testbench to verify the correctness of the adder.

Write a Verilog HDL module to implement a 4-bit decimal adder. The decimal adder should accept two decimal numbers (e.g., 15 and 20) as inputs and produce the sum (30) as output. The module should also include a carry-out signal to indicate if a carry from the next most significant bit is available.
questions.hint: Use intermediate binary representations to facilitate the conversion and calculation.

questions.question: Design a Verilog HDL module to implement a decimal to binary converter that can be used to convert decimal numbers to binary for decimal addition. The module should accept a decimal input (e.g., 12) and produce a 4-bit binary representation (1100) as output.
questions.hint: Use a 4-bit binary register to store the binary representation of the decimal number.
